description: An aerospace history group is creating a digital twin of the Concorde. The aerodynamics team needs a parametric baseline of the main wing. Read the specification document at ~/Desktop/concorde_wing_spec.txt. In OpenVSP, create a new model with a Wing component named 'Concorde_Wing'. Set the planform parameters (Span: 25.6m, Root Chord: 34.0m, Tip Chord: 2.0m, Leading Edge Sweep: 60.0 degrees) in the Plan tab. Then, navigate to the XSec (Cross Section) tab. For BOTH the root and tip sections, change the Airfoil Type from the default subsonic NACA shape to 'Biconvex' (a supersonic profile) and set the Thickness-to-Chord (T/C) ratio to 0.03 (3%). Save the completed model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/concorde_wing.vsp3.
